description.short: Measures production equipment effectiveness from availability, performance, and quality inputs.
description.long: Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) is a critical metric for how effectively production assets are used. This calculator uses planned production time, downtime, ideal cycle time, and part counts to compute OEE and surface downtime loss drivers. It follows ISO 22400, Lean manufacturing, and Six Sigma guidance for shop-floor reviews.

short_i18n.en: Measures production equipment effectiveness from availability, performance, and quality inputs.
short_i18n.tr: Kullanılabilirlik, performans ve kalite girdileriyle üretim ekipmanı etkinliğini ölçer.

long_i18n.en: Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) is a critical metric for how effectively production assets are used. This calculator uses planned production time, downtime, ideal cycle time, and part counts to compute OEE and surface downtime loss drivers. It follows ISO 22400, Lean manufacturing, and Six Sigma guidance for shop-floor reviews.
long_i18n.tr: OEE (Genel Ekipman Etkinliği), üretim ekipmanlarının ne kadar etkin kullanıldığını gösteren kritik bir metriktir. Bu hesaplayıcı planlanan üretim süresi, duruş süreleri, ideal çevrim süresi ve üretilen parça sayısı gibi girdilerle OEE'yi hesaplar ve duruş kaynaklarını analiz eder. ISO 22400, Lean üretim ve Six Sigma rehberliğiyle uyumludur.

example.title: OEE improvement on a CNC shop floor
example.scenario: An 8-hour shift (480 minutes) has 60 minutes of unplanned downtime. Ideal cycle time is 0.5 minutes per part. The shift produces 180 parts, 15 of which are defective.
example.result: OEE is 17.2%, indicating major speed and downtime losses. Reducing downtime or improving cycle time against the ideal rate should be the first improvement priority.

title_i18n.en: OEE improvement on a CNC shop floor

scenario_i18n.en: An 8-hour shift (480 minutes) has 60 minutes of unplanned downtime. Ideal cycle time is 0.5 minutes per part. The shift produces 180 parts, 15 of which are defective.
scenario_i18n.tr: 8 saatlik bir vardiyada (480 dakika) toplam 60 dakika plansız duruş yaşanıyor. İdeal çevrim süresi parça başına 0.5 dakika. Vardiya boyunca 180 parça üretiliyor, bunlardan 15'i hatalı.

result_i18n.en: OEE is 17.2%, indicating major speed and downtime losses. Reducing downtime or improving cycle time against the ideal rate should be the first improvement priority.
result_i18n.tr: OEE %17,2 olarak hesaplanır. Bu, ciddi hız ve duruş kayıpları olduğunu gösterir. Duruş sürelerini azaltmak veya ideal çevrim süresine yaklaşmak ilk iyileştirme önceliği olmalıdır.

faqs.question: What is OEE?
faqs.answer: OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) measures equipment productivity as the product of availability, performance, and quality.

answer_i18n.en: OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) measures equipment productivity as the product of availability, performance, and quality.
answer_i18n.tr: OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness), ekipman etkinliğini kullanılabilirlik, performans ve kalite faktörlerinin çarpımıyla ölçen bir metriktir.

faqs.question: How is ideal cycle time determined?
faqs.answer: Ideal cycle time is the shortest time required to produce one good part under optimal conditions, often from OEM data or best demonstrated shift records.

question_i18n.en: How is ideal cycle time determined?

answer_i18n.en: Ideal cycle time is the shortest time required to produce one good part under optimal conditions, often from OEM data or best demonstrated shift records.
answer_i18n.tr: İdeal çevrim süresi, bir parçanın optimum koşullarda üretilmesi için gereken en kısa süredir. Genellikle makine üreticisinin verileri veya en iyi performans gösteren vardiya kayıtlarından belirlenir.

inputs.label: Planned Production Time (minutes)
inputs.businessContext: Total scheduled time for production in a shift/day (e.g., 8 hours = 480 minutes).

businessContext_i18n.en: Total scheduled time for production in a shift/day (e.g., 8 hours = 480 minutes).

inputs.label: Total Downtime (minutes)
inputs.businessContext: Sum of all unplanned and planned stops (breakdowns, setup, maintenance).

businessContext_i18n.en: Sum of all unplanned and planned stops (breakdowns, setup, maintenance).

inputs.label: Ideal Cycle Time (minutes per unit)
inputs.businessContext: Theoretical fastest time to produce one unit under optimal conditions.

businessContext_i18n.en: Theoretical fastest time to produce one unit under optimal conditions.

inputs.label: Total Parts Produced
inputs.businessContext: Total count of units produced during the planned production time.

businessContext_i18n.en: Total count of units produced during the planned production time.

inputs.label: Defective Parts
inputs.businessContext: Number of units that do not meet quality standards (scrap, rework).

businessContext_i18n.en: Number of units that do not meet quality standards (scrap, rework).

inputs.label: Shift Type
inputs.businessContext: Type of shift to adjust for fatigue or environmental factors.

businessContext_i18n.en: Type of shift to adjust for fatigue or environmental factors.

inputs.label: Include Micro-Stops (< 2 min) in Downtime
inputs.businessContext: If true, small stops are counted as downtime per Six Sigma guidelines.

businessContext_i18n.en: If true, small stops are counted as downtime per Six Sigma guidelines.

rules.message: Downtime cannot exceed planned production time.
Defective parts must be less than or equal to total parts produced.
Ideal cycle time must be greater than zero.

thresholds.message: Quality below 99% – defect rate too high.

suggestedActions: Review inputs and verify results against site standards.
